name: transfer_ibft_raw
description: Raw IBFT (Interbank Fund Transfer) event log from core banking gateway. Source-of-truth — preserves EVERY status transition, retry attempt, intermediate state, and raw bank payload exactly as received. Includes events the warehouse drops (failed, pending, reversed, duplicate). Data Lake benefit: full event granularity for root-cause debugging, audit trail, and discovering edge cases not yet modeled in warehouse.
best_for: Debugging failed/stuck transfers (full retry history + bank error codes), inspecting raw_payload for reconciliation with the bank, root-cause analysis when KPI numbers look wrong, discovering new fields/metrics before they reach the warehouse, data engineering investigation.
not_for: KPI reporting or BI dashboards — numbers will diverge from official reports because raw events include retries, pending, and failed attempts. Fast SQL aggregation — Spark/Trino is slower than Redshift; use fact_transfer_ibft instead.

name: fact_transfer_ibft
description: Curated IBFT fact table. One row per SUCCESSFUL transfer (plus first-final-fail), deduplicated, with conformed bank/channel/user dimensions. ETL applies business rules (final-status only, test users filtered, amounts normalized). Data Warehouse benefit: numbers match official BI reports, queries are fast (Redshift columnar), dimensions are pre-joined — ready for dashboards without SQL gymnastics.
best_for: Official KPI reporting (total transfer volume, success rate, fee revenue), monthly business reports, BI dashboards (Tableau/Metabase/Superset), executive summaries, joins with dim_merchants/dim_users for segment analysis.
not_for: Debugging individual failed transfers (final-status only — intermediate retry/error events are filtered out, use transfer_ibft_raw on lake). Investigating raw bank payload or new fields not yet in the curated schema.

name: transfer_ibft_daily_agg
description: Pre-aggregated daily IBFT volume and value by beneficiary bank. Mart layer purpose-built for dashboards and trend charts — eliminates the need to GROUP BY at query time. Data Warehouse (mart) benefit: sub-second dashboard refresh, consistent numbers across all consumers, no aggregation logic duplicated in BI tools.
best_for: Dashboard charts (daily trend volume IBFT, bank mix), executive reporting, time-series visualization, quick SUM/AVG by bank.
not_for: Anything below daily grain or per-user analysis (this is aggregated — go to fact_transfer_ibft for per-transfer grain). Debugging individual transfers.

name: appsflyer_raw
description: Raw AppsFlyer mobile attribution event stream: installs, re-engagements, in-app events, postbacks. Preserves full raw attribution payload, device fingerprinting fields, and unmodified postback body from AppsFlyer. Data Lake benefit: enables fraud detection on raw installs, attribution model experimentation, and access to fields/dimensions that haven't been promoted to the warehouse yet.
best_for: Mobile attribution fraud detection (device fingerprint + IP patterns), investigating specific campaign postbacks, raw event-value parsing for new attribution models, deep-dive on re-attribution windows.
not_for: Standard marketing KPI (CPI/ROAS/install count) — use fact_appsflyer_events. Quick aggregate queries — Spark/Trino is slower than Redshift here.

name: fact_appsflyer_events
description: Curated AppsFlyer mobile attribution events. Deduplicated installs, normalized campaign hierarchy, joined with internal user table. ETL hourly. Data Warehouse benefit: marketing KPI numbers consistent across all dashboards, no need to dedup installs yourself, campaign hierarchy already conformed.
best_for: Marketing KPI (install count, CPI, ROAS, retention by source), campaign performance dashboards, attribution reports, user acquisition funnel.
not_for: Fraud investigation needing raw device fingerprint or IP (use appsflyer_raw). Access to unmodeled postback fields.

name: payment_with_wallet_raw
description: Raw e-wallet payment transaction log. Captures EVERY payment attempt — pending, in-flight, failed, reversed, idempotent retries — with full merchant payload, device context, and raw response from the payment processor. Data Lake benefit: full transaction lifecycle visibility for fraud/reconciliation, and raw_response/device_id enable forensic analysis impossible in the warehouse.
best_for: Debugging failed payments (full lifecycle + processor response), fraud investigation with device context, reconciling with merchant statements, discovering new payment failure patterns, idempotency-key audits.
not_for: GMV/revenue reporting (use fact_payment_wallet — numbers will not match BI dashboards if you sum raw events). Quick SQL aggregations.

name: fact_payment_wallet
description: Curated wallet payment fact table. One row per completed payment (success or final-fail). Conformed merchant_category, user_segment, channel dimensions joined in. Data Warehouse benefit: GMV/revenue numbers match the official monthly P&L, merchant attributes pre-joined, ready for slice-and-dice in BI tools.
best_for: GMV metrics, payment success rate dashboard, merchant performance KPI, transaction volume reporting, revenue analytics, category-level cohort analysis.
not_for: Investigating specific failed/pending transactions or device-level fraud signals — use payment_with_wallet_raw on lake.

name: transactions_raw
description: Master raw transaction log across all transaction types: transfer, payment, top-up, withdrawal. Never deduped, includes every state transition, all currencies, all channels. Data Lake benefit: the single most complete ledger view — required for cross-product investigations and full financial reconciliation that no single fact table can answer alone.
best_for: Cross-product deep-dive, full transaction lifecycle reconstruction, raw ledger audit, anomaly pattern discovery spanning all transaction types, financial reconciliation between products.
not_for: Business KPI reporting — use fact_transactions for consistent numbers. Single-product analysis — use the product-specific fact tables for cleaner slicing.

name: fact_transactions
description: Unified curated transaction fact table across all product types. Deduplicated, final-status only, conformed dimensions for product, channel, and user_segment. Data Warehouse benefit: cross-product KPI in a single table — DAU/MAU and total volume queryable with one GROUP BY, numbers consistent with executive dashboards.
best_for: Total transaction volume across all products, daily active transacting users (DATU), cross-product performance dashboard, executive summary KPI, monthly business reporting.
not_for: Cross-product reconciliation between this and product-specific facts — use transactions_raw on lake to avoid silent ETL drift.

name: top_up_raw
description: Raw top-up (wallet reload) event log from all channels: bank transfer, card, convenience store, agent. Includes full gateway response, retry events, and pending states. Data Lake benefit: see WHY a top-up fails (gateway error code + retry chain), not just THAT it failed — essential for channel team to diagnose partner issues.
best_for: Debugging top-up failures by channel/bank, gateway response code analysis, retry history, finding users with repeated failed top-ups, new channel rollout monitoring.
not_for: Top-up KPI reporting (volume, channel mix). Use fact_top_up — raw includes failed/retry rows that will inflate counts.

name: fact_top_up
description: Curated top-up fact table. Successful top-ups only (plus first-final-fail). Conformed channel and bank dimensions joined in. Data Warehouse benefit: clean channel mix and reload volume KPI ready for daily/monthly reporting — no need to filter retries or normalize bank codes.
best_for: Top-up volume KPI, channel mix dashboard, daily/monthly reload trend, top-up fee revenue, bank channel performance metric.
not_for: Channel/gateway debugging (use top_up_raw for full error codes and retries).

name: p2p_transfer_raw
description: Raw peer-to-peer wallet transfer event log. Every send-money event between users including pending, success, fail, reversed. Includes device_id, IP, and fraud signals from the risk engine. Data Lake benefit: social-graph + device-graph analysis impossible in warehouse — essential for catching money-mule networks and abuse.
best_for: Fraud investigation on P2P transfers, device/IP pattern analysis, full lifecycle including reversals, social graph analysis, money-mule pattern discovery.
not_for: P2P volume KPI or DAU metrics — use fact_p2p_transfer (raw includes reversed/failed events that distort counts).

name: fact_p2p_transfer
description: Curated P2P transfer fact table. Final-status events only. Conformed sender/receiver user dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: clean P2P send-money KPI ready for product reporting; reversed/failed events already filtered.
best_for: P2P volume KPI, daily send-money trend, active sender/receiver counts, P2P revenue dashboard, product manager reporting.
not_for: Fraud/abuse investigation — use p2p_transfer_raw for device, IP, and fraud_signal columns.

name: qr_payments_raw
description: Raw QR code payment event stream: scan events, payment attempts, QR validation steps, terminal info, full merchant POS payload, acquirer response. Data Lake benefit: scan-to-payment funnel visibility (warehouse only sees completed payments — you lose the drop-off insight needed for product/UX work).
best_for: Debugging QR scan-to-payment drop-off, terminal error investigation, POS integration issues, acquirer response code analysis, QR payment fraud investigation.
not_for: QR GMV / success rate dashboards — use fact_qr_payments (raw includes scan-only events with no payment, which will deflate success rate).

name: fact_qr_payments
description: Curated QR payment fact table. Successful and final-status QR payments with conformed merchant_category, city, and user_segment dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: GMV-by-city and merchant-category slicing one GROUP BY away — no joins to dim_merchants needed.
best_for: QR payment volume KPI, success rate dashboard, merchant category spending analysis, GMV by city, QR product performance reporting.
not_for: Scan-to-payment funnel analysis or POS error debugging — use qr_payments_raw.

name: bill_payment_raw
description: Raw bill payment event log: utilities, telecom, insurance, loans. Includes full biller API response, retry events, intermediate states from biller gateway. Data Lake benefit: biller-specific error codes and raw responses for partner reconciliation — the warehouse strips these in favor of normalized status.
best_for: Biller integration debugging, biller-specific error code investigation, settlement reconciliation with billers, discovering new biller failure patterns.
not_for: Bill payment KPI / biller performance dashboards — use fact_bill_payment for cleaned final-status data.

name: fact_bill_payment
description: Curated bill payment fact table. Final-status payments with conformed biller and bill_type dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: ready-to-query biller performance KPI without parsing raw API responses; biller names and types already normalized.
best_for: Bill payment volume KPI, biller performance dashboard, utility payment trends, fee revenue from bills, monthly reporting by biller type.
not_for: Biller-side debugging or reconciliation — use bill_payment_raw.

name: login_events_raw
description: Raw user login and authentication event stream: OTP sent/verified, biometric auth, session creation, logout, forced logout. Includes device fingerprint, geolocation, app_version. Data Lake benefit: security/risk teams get full event granularity (OTP failures, device switches, suspicious IP changes) impossible to reconstruct from session-grain warehouse data.
best_for: User behavior deep-dive, security investigation, login anomaly detection, device switching analysis, OTP failure patterns, UX friction analysis in login flow.
not_for: DAU/MAU/engagement KPI — use fact_user_sessions (raw events do not equal sessions; multiple events per session).

name: fact_user_sessions
description: Curated user session fact table. One row per completed session with duration, auth method, and user_segment. ETL hourly. Data Warehouse benefit: session is the canonical engagement unit — DAU/MAU/retention calculable with simple SQL, numbers match the engagement dashboards used by Product.
best_for: DAU/MAU metrics, session duration dashboard, auth method adoption KPI, user engagement reporting, retention analysis.
not_for: Security/fraud investigation needing device fingerprint, IP, geo — use login_events_raw.

name: kyc_events_raw
description: Raw KYC verification event stream: document upload, OCR result, face matching, liveness check, manual review steps. Includes raw OCR payload and vendor response. Data Lake benefit: compliance/onboarding teams can audit every step of the funnel and OCR confidence scores per vendor — warehouse only keeps final tier outcome.
best_for: KYC funnel deep-dive (step-level drop-off), OCR accuracy analysis per vendor, manual review queue investigation, compliance audit trail, discovering drop-off points in onboarding flow.
not_for: KYC approval rate KPI — use fact_kyc_verification for the canonical numbers.

name: fact_kyc_verification
description: Curated KYC verification outcomes. One row per user's final KYC attempt result with pass/fail and tier assigned. Data Warehouse benefit: compliance KPI ready for monthly regulator reporting — final outcome only, deduplicated per user.
best_for: KYC approval rate KPI, rejection reason dashboard, onboarding funnel metrics, compliance reporting, user tier distribution.
not_for: Step-level funnel analysis or vendor OCR debugging — use kyc_events_raw.

name: refund_chargeback_raw
description: Raw refund and chargeback request event stream. Every state transition, dispute communication, and raw bank response for chargebacks. Data Lake benefit: dispute team can replay the entire case timeline and bank exchange — warehouse only has the final resolution.
best_for: Dispute case investigation, chargeback root-cause analysis, bank response code deep-dive, refund SLA investigation, discovering recurring refund patterns.
not_for: Refund-rate / chargeback-ratio KPI — use fact_refund_chargeback.

name: fact_refund_chargeback
description: Curated refund and chargeback fact table. Final resolution status with conformed reason and product dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: risk team gets clean refund rate / chargeback ratio for loss reporting — no need to filter intermediate dispute states.
best_for: Refund rate KPI, chargeback ratio dashboard, dispute resolution SLA metrics, loss rate reporting, risk team reporting.
not_for: Individual dispute case investigation — use refund_chargeback_raw.

name: card_transactions_raw
description: Raw virtual/physical card transaction log from card network (Visa/MC). Includes authorization, clearing, settlement steps and full ISO 8583 message fields. Data Lake benefit: card-ops team can decode ISO 8583 fields directly and reconcile clearing vs settlement gaps invisible to the warehouse.
best_for: Card authorization failure debugging, ISO 8583 message inspection, raw MCC analysis, card fraud investigation, clearing-vs-settlement reconciliation.
not_for: Card spending KPI / interchange fee revenue reporting — use fact_card_transactions.

name: fact_card_transactions
description: Curated card transaction fact table. Settled transactions with decoded MCC, conformed merchant_category and user_segment dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: card spending and interchange revenue queryable without ISO 8583 parsing; merchant category already mapped.
best_for: Card spending KPI, merchant category spending dashboard, card GMV reporting, interchange fee revenue, card product manager report.
not_for: Authorization-stage debugging, ISO 8583 inspection — use card_transactions_raw.

name: promo_redemption_raw
description: Raw promotional campaign redemption event stream: voucher applications, cashback calculations, eligibility checks, reward disbursements. Includes the full eligibility rule evaluation trace. Data Lake benefit: promo team can audit WHY a user did or did not qualify (rule_eval_log) — warehouse only records the final disbursement.
best_for: Cashback eligibility rule debugging, promo abuse investigation, full eligibility trace, edge-case discovery in new campaigns.
not_for: Campaign ROI / cashback redemption rate KPI — use fact_promo_redemption.

name: fact_promo_redemption
description: Curated promotional redemption fact table. One row per successful cashback/discount with campaign and user_segment dimensions. Data Warehouse benefit: campaign ROI and cashback cost reporting ready for monthly marketing spend reviews — no need to filter ineligible attempts.
best_for: Cashback redemption rate KPI, campaign ROI dashboard, promo cost reporting, monthly marketing spend, user segment cashback analysis, promotion performance report.
not_for: Eligibility rule debugging or abuse investigation — use promo_redemption_raw.

name: dim_merchants
description: Merchant master dimension table. All registered merchants with category, location, status, tier. Slowly Changing Dimension (Type 2). Data Warehouse benefit: the canonical merchant join key — every payment fact table uses this. Pre-deduped, sanctioned by the merchant ops team.
best_for: Merchant lookup, joins with payment fact tables, merchant segmentation, merchant reporting, category analysis.
not_for: Merchant onboarding step-level investigation — that lives in the raw onboarding events (talk to merchant ops).

name: fx_rates_daily
description: Daily foreign exchange rates for all supported currencies. Sourced from central bank and payment network rates. Used for multi-currency transaction conversion. Data Warehouse benefit: a single sanctioned FX source so cross-currency KPI are consistent across all reports.
best_for: Currency conversion in reports, multi-currency KPI normalization, FX rate lookup, international transaction reporting.
not_for: Intra-day FX timing analysis — this is end-of-day only.

name: merchant_settlement_raw
description: Raw merchant settlement batch files and disbursement events. Contains full settlement batch payload, fee deductions, bank transfer confirmation, gross/net breakdown. Data Lake benefit: finance reconciliation team gets the raw bank-statement-level detail needed for cent-perfect settlement audits.
best_for: Settlement reconciliation deep-dive, missing-settlement investigation, bank transfer failure analysis, net fee calculation audit, finance team reconciliation.
not_for: Settlement KPI / payout dashboards — use fact_merchant_settlement.

name: fact_merchant_settlement
description: Curated merchant settlement fact table. Daily settled amounts per merchant with fee breakdown. Data Warehouse benefit: payout reporting in one query — gross, fee, net already aggregated and joined with dim_merchants.
best_for: Merchant settlement KPI, daily payout dashboard, fee revenue reporting, finance team settlement summary, merchant finance reporting.
not_for: Bank-level reconciliation or missing-batch investigation — use merchant_settlement_raw.

name: fraud_alerts_summary
description: Aggregated fraud alert summary from the fraud detection engine. Daily rule-triggered alerts by type, risk score distributions, and resolution outcomes. Data Warehouse (mart) benefit: risk monitoring dashboard ready out-of-the-box; alert counts already deduped by case_id.
best_for: Fraud KPI dashboard, risk monitoring, alert volume trend, false-positive rate reporting, risk management executive summary.
not_for: Per-alert investigation or rule replay — that requires the raw fraud engine logs (talk to risk-analytics).

name: user_events_raw
description: Raw user lifecycle event stream from operational DB (CDC): account creation, profile updates (name, email, phone, address), status changes (active/suspended/closed), tier upgrades, preference changes. One row per change event. Data Lake benefit: full audit history of every profile attribute change — critical for compliance (KYC re-verification triggers), customer-support escalations, and PII change forensics.
best_for: Audit trail of profile changes, investigating suspicious account-takeover patterns, GDPR/PII compliance requests, customer-support escalations needing 'what changed and when'.
not_for: Current user attributes for dashboards — use dim_users (this is event history, not the current state).

name: dim_users
description: User master dimension table (Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2). Current and historical user attributes: name (masked for non-privileged users), age_band, gender, city, segment, kyc_tier, account_status, registration_date. Data Warehouse benefit: the canonical user join key for every fact table; PII is masked according to policy, ready for analyst use without manual redaction.
best_for: User segmentation in dashboards, joins with payment/transaction fact tables, cohort analysis, demographic reporting, audience sizing for marketing.
not_for: Auditing 'who changed my email and when' — use user_events_raw. Accessing un-masked PII — that needs privileged warehouse roles, not this table.

name: dim_banks
description: Bank reference dimension. All partner banks supported by the wallet: bank_code, bank_name, swift_code, country, status (active/suspended), card_supported, transfer_supported. Maintained by partnerships team. Data Warehouse benefit: a single sanctioned source for bank attributes — used in every IBFT/top-up/card report so bank names are consistent.
best_for: Bank name lookup in reports, partner status filtering, joining bank_code → bank_name in transfer/top-up dashboards.
not_for: Tracking bank integration health or partner outage events — that lives in operational monitoring, not the catalog.

name: notification_events_raw
description: Raw outbound notification event stream: push notifications, SMS, email, in-app messages. Captures send-attempt, vendor response (FCM/APNs/SMS gateway), delivery status, user open/click events. Data Lake benefit: full message lifecycle including vendor failure codes — needed to debug 'why didn't my OTP arrive' and channel-specific delivery rate by carrier.
best_for: Debugging undelivered notifications (OTP, marketing push), vendor failure rate analysis by carrier/region, A/B test message content analysis, GDPR opt-out audit.
not_for: Notification volume / open rate KPI dashboards — use fact_notifications.

name: fact_notifications
description: Curated notification fact table. One row per delivered notification with channel, template, delivery_status, open/click flags. Data Warehouse benefit: notification KPI (delivery rate, open rate, click rate) ready for marketing/CRM dashboards without filtering vendor-level retries.
best_for: Notification volume KPI, open/click rate dashboards, CRM campaign performance, channel mix reporting (push vs SMS vs email), marketing engagement KPI.
not_for: Debugging undelivered messages or vendor failure analysis — use notification_events_raw.

name: cs_interaction_raw
description: Raw customer-support interaction event stream: ticket creation, agent assignment, chat messages, call transcripts, status changes, resolution notes. Includes full message body and call recording metadata. Data Lake benefit: CS team and QA can replay the entire conversation timeline — warehouse only keeps ticket-level aggregates.
best_for: QA audit of CS conversations, root-cause analysis of high-escalation tickets, NLP/sentiment training data, voice-of-customer extraction, agent coaching.
not_for: CS KPI dashboards (CSAT, average handle time, FCR) — use fact_cs_interaction.

name: fact_cs_interaction
description: Curated CS interaction fact table. One row per resolved ticket with handle_time, channel, primary_intent, resolution_status, csat_score. Data Warehouse benefit: CS performance KPI (CSAT, AHT, FCR, escalation rate) directly queryable for ops dashboards.
best_for: CSAT dashboard, average handle time KPI, first-contact-resolution rate, agent performance ranking, ticket volume by intent, CS ops monthly reports.
not_for: Conversation content analysis, agent coaching, NLP training — use cs_interaction_raw.

name: cashier_app_events_raw
description: Raw event stream from merchant cashier (POS) app: cashier login, QR generation, scan-customer-QR, void, refund initiation, end-of-day close, sync errors. Includes device_id, app_version, GPS location. Data Lake benefit: merchant ops team can debug exactly which screen/step the cashier struggles with — warehouse only knows the resulting transaction.
best_for: Cashier UX debugging, sync-error investigation, training-need discovery (steps where cashiers frequently abandon), merchant onboarding success diagnostics, version-rollout impact analysis.
not_for: Cashier productivity KPI dashboards — use fact_cashier_activity.

name: fact_cashier_activity
description: Curated cashier activity fact table. Daily per-cashier metrics: transactions_processed, voids, refunds, active_hours, app_version. Data Warehouse benefit: merchant ops gets productivity KPI per cashier/per merchant ready for partner reviews — no need to deduplicate sync retries.
best_for: Cashier productivity KPI, per-merchant cashier ranking, app version adoption tracking, merchant partner business reviews.
not_for: Cashier UX debugging or step-level investigation — use cashier_app_events_raw.
